channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.mmorpg.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.mmorpg.com

item.title: Final Fantasy XI Celebrates 24th 'Vana'versary' This Month With Lots of Bonuses and Special Campaigns
item.content:encoded:
Final Fantasy XI is celebrating its 24th anniversary of release in Japan, better known as Vana'versary, this month, and Square Enix has announced a special anniversary campaign with events, better drop rates, and of course, rewards and special bonuses.

The festivities begin on May 13th, with the start of the anniversary campaign. Final Fantasy XI continues to have a robust community. Additional bonuses will roll out in phases throughout the event, which will end on June 9th. This phase includes a chain experience, Capacity Point, and Monstrosity campaign bonuses. Experience chains will yield double to triple the usual amount of XP and capacity points. There will also be double synthesis skill increases, a special campaign Festa with double XP and allied notes.

There are also opportunities to engrave your gear with a similar effect to using dark matter, a Bonus Bayld campaign, increased seal and crest drop rates, and a special Seal Battlefield Spoils Campaign. Those are just the things coming in the first phase and the team is kicking things off big. On May 17th, phase one gets a special bonus round with even more goodies.

Phase 2 kicks off on May 24th with more XP bonuses, more bonus campaigns, and a lot more  to take advantage of. Phases 3 and 4 are both intentionally loaded up with special bonuses. Most of these continue but there are some new  offerings in these two phases with the final one opening June 5th.

If you are leveling characters or you want to roll a new one, these anniversary campaign bonuses are ideal for that. additionally, if you want to improve your gear, pursue new goals, and even take on some new stuff, this is also for you

Of course, if you enjoy hanging out in Vana’diel, you can figure out which of these bonuses are most fun to acquire. This month, the Maiden's Rhapsody collab returns to Final Fantasy XIV.

You can check out the full listing in the anniversary announcement.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/final-fantasy-xi-celebrates-24th-vanaversary-this-month-with-lots-of-bonuses-and-special-campaigns-2000137974
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Pantheon: Rise of the Fallen's Combat and Progression PTR Opens This Week
item.content:encoded:
Pantheon: Rise of the Fallen’s Combat and Progression update public test realm will open this Wednesday. The PTR will launch with an early test build and add more of the huge series of updates and overhauls Visionary Realms plans to get the game into a 1.0 ready state.

The team laid out the details of what will be available in the first week, and how they plan to progress. In the first week, eight classes will be available to level 10. Also in are unified NPC templates, character attribute updates, including base skill updates (but no Mastery yet),  the new experience curve, and rested experience bonus, as well as the updated combat formulas. This first build also includes the first to engage tagging system (with the rules to help limit abuse of the system), bind on equip items, and the first iteration of the mail system.

They want feedback on everything and the whole experience, all of the new systems, and anything you might encounter but they also have a few specific areas they want to hear back on. For the first week that includes whether solo and small group difficulty feels right, feedback on the first to engage mechanics,  progression, how does rested experience feel, and general combat, among more specific questions you'll find in the PTR announcement.  the announcement also gives info on some of their plans for updates, data gathering, and more of how they're putting this huge update together.

The team is planning for new PTR updates every Wednesday.  Future systems that we'll see in later PTR updates include the Mastery system, the market system, spell pushback, updated class mechanics as well as additional classes, boats, and full crafting and gathering progression.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/pantheon-rise-of-the-fallens-combat-and-progression-ptr-opens-this-week-2000137973
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
